title: mentha amplexicaulis
intro: Mentha amplexicaulis, also known as the large leafed mint or creeping mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. It is a hardy, spreading plant with large, oval-shaped leaves and purple flowers, often used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The essential oil extracted from Mentha amplexicaulis is rich in menthol and menthone, which have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, and is traditionally used to treat digestive issues, headaches, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Mentha amplexicaulis include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as provide relief from muscle and joint pain.
